# UC-17.1.75 · ISE Downloadable ACL (dACL) Push Failures and ACL-Length Truncation Detection

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Operations, Security &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*ISE pushes access rules to switches when a user connects. If the rule is too big or the switch can't accept it, the rule may be cut short — and the user gets less restricted than intended. We watch for these silent rule failures.*

---

## Description

Detects: (a) dACLs that fail to push to switches (incompatible IOS, ACL syntax errors). (b) dACLs that are truncated by switches with limited TCAM (older Catalyst with shallow ACL TCAM).

## Value

Truncated dACLs silently weaken access control — the policy in ISE may allow X but the switch enforces Y. Catching dACL push failures lets policy engineering correct ACL design.

## Implementation

Schedule the search every 1 hour over the last 24 hours; trigger when dACL drift is observed; throttle by `dacl_name` for 24 hours.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Push an oversized dACL to an old switch. UC-17.1.75 detects 5438 truncation.

### Negative scenario

Reasonable dACLs to current switches. No fires.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (5437, 5438)
| stats count by dacl_name, nas_ip, MessageCode, failure_reason
| where count > 5
```

## Visualization

dACL push failure trend; per-dACL failure heatmap.

## Known False Positives

- Intermittent push failures during NAD reboot.
- Older Catalyst 2960 series have known ACL-length limits.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — Downloadable ACLs](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/security/ise/3-4/admin_guide/b_ise_admin_3_4/m_dot1x_implementation.html)
